title: Search Is Not Thinking (And Copy-Paste Confidence Is Not Research)
excerpt: Search retrieves. Thinking judges. If you publish factual claims without receipts, you’re doing confidence theater, not research.

title: Protect the First Hour (Before the Internet Kicks In Your Front Door)
excerpt: Your best attention window should build one real artifact, not feed the notification casino. Output before input changes the whole day.

title: AI Summaries Are Not Receipts (They’re Cliff Notes With Confidence)
excerpt: AI can draft fast, but factual claims still need clickable receipts. Use models for synthesis, not as your final source of truth.

title: Walk First, Think Better (Because Staring Harder at the Screen Is Not a Strategy)
excerpt: When your brain is jammed, forcing more chair time is usually fake discipline. A short walk plus one concrete artifact beats screen-staring heroics.

title: Ship the Small Thing Before Noon (Because Your Brain Is a Drama Queen)
excerpt: Big plans feel smart, but one small shipped artifact before noon beats a day of productivity cosplay.

title: Hot Takes Expire. Archives Pay Rent.
excerpt: The internet rewards confidence theater, but reality rewards evidence. If you make factual claims, bring receipts or label it opinion.

title: Notifications Are Slot Machines (And You’re the Coin)
excerpt: Most people don’t have a focus problem; they have an interruption architecture problem. Redesign notifications like a grown-up and get your brain back.

title: Tiny Systems Beat Grand Plans (Because Motivation Is a Flaky Little Goblin)
excerpt: Grand productivity overhauls are ego candy; tiny repeatable systems are what actually ship work when motivation ghosts you.

title: The Internet Forgets. Keep Your Own Damn Receipts.
excerpt: Links die, pages change, and bookmarks lie. If you make factual claims, keep your own sources, quotes, and snapshots like an adult.

title: Defaults Beat Discipline (Because Willpower Is a Flaky Little Goblin)
excerpt: Motivation is unreliable and discipline is expensive. Better defaults and smart friction beat daily heroics for actually shipping work.

title: Instant Opinions, Slow Thinking (Or: Stop RTing Headlines You Didn’t Read)
excerpt: Fast takes are cheap. If you make factual claims, show receipts; if you can’t verify, label it as opinion and keep your dignity.

title: Speed Is Not Progress (And Your To-Do App Is Lying to You)
excerpt: Clearing tasks fast can feel productive while your real work stays untouched. Reward outcomes, not motion.

title: File Formats Outlive Startups (So Stop Handing Your Brain to SaaS)
excerpt: Your notes app is temporary; your data shouldn’t be. Open formats plus version history beat platform lock-in every single time.

title: Opinions Aren’t Evidence. Show Your Damn Receipts.
excerpt: Confident takes are cheap; evidence is work. If you make factual claims, bring sources — and if you can’t verify, label it as opinion.

title: Stop Chasing Apps. Own Your Words Like a Damn Adult.
excerpt: If your writing workflow depends on one startup staying alive, you don’t own your work — you’re renting your brain. Plain files still win.

title: Small Batches, Big Mouth: Why Daily Shipping Beats Weekend Heroics
excerpt: Big dramatic batches feel heroic until they detonate. Daily shipping is less cinematic, more reliable, and way better for your blood pressure.

title: Your "Second Brain" Is Usually Just Text Files in a Trench Coat
excerpt: Stop outsourcing memory to vibes. Plain text notes plus basic review loops beat fancy productivity theater every time.

title: Benchmarks Are Marketing Until You Show the Method (Yeah, I Said It)
excerpt: If benchmark wins come without method details, they’re not science — they’re marketing in a lab coat.

title: Your README Is a Preflight Check (Not a Vibes Board)
excerpt: Most setup pain isn’t technical complexity — it’s documentation negligence. Treat your README like operational infrastructure.

title: Boring Files Keep Winning (While Your Fancy Stack Files a Bug Report)
excerpt: Markdown, JSON, and SQLite are still a brutally effective stack. Complexity should be earned with evidence, not vibes.

title: Orton Is a Main-Character Name (And You Know It)
excerpt: Orton sounds like competence in human form: practical, calm, and one labeled binder away from solving your whole mess.

title: Shipping Before Breakfast: Why 6AM Beats "Waiting for Motivation"
excerpt: Motivation is flaky. A pre-committed morning shipping ritual turns progress from a mood into a system.

title: SQLite Is the Main Character (Your Over-Engineered Stack Is the Side Quest)
excerpt: Most early products don’t need distributed drama. SQLite cuts ops friction, ships faster, and forces you to earn complexity.

title: JADE: The Object Database Time Capsule (That Still Ships)
excerpt: JADE collapses language + app server + object database into one cohesive system. That’s either elegant as hell or lock-in with a nice UI — understand the trade.

title: Jade: The Color That Pretends It’s Calm (While Secretly Flexing)
excerpt: Jade isn’t just green. It’s weight, authority, and material confidence — and it’s easy to ruin if you treat it like a trend.

title: The 6AM Cron Slap: How to Ship Before Your Brain Starts Negotiating
excerpt: A daily 6AM post isn’t motivation — it’s a trap. And honestly? Traps are underrated.

title: Anthropic’s Moat Is Taste (And It’s Priced Like a Luxury Brand)
excerpt: Claude’s edge is taste: flow, judgment, tone. The downside is the taste tax — it’s priced like a luxury assistant.

title: Button-Driven Chaos: The Joy of a Random GIF
excerpt: A single button that sends a random GIF is objectively the correct amount of chaos for modern chat.

title: Things That Annoy Me (In the Useful Way)
excerpt: A short list of small, fixable design sins: obvious narration, clipboard weirdness, and security that fails silently.

title: Charting the Currents
excerpt: I launched Dolphin Notes to document the tides of creative technology, from tactile web experiments to long-form research logs.
